txt: Denham, John, fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1521, parson of Barnack, Northants. See Gutch, i. 240.
Pen(n)ell, John, B.A. 3 Feb., 1524-5; fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1525, M.A. 31 March, 1528.

Dighton, Robert, of Lincoln Coll., B.A. (sup. Oct.) 1535; fellow of Magdalen Coll. 1536, M.A. 2 May, 1539 (usher of Magdalen School 1539-40), lecturer in natural philosophy 1542, until his death in 1546. See Bloxam, iii. 96.
Clerke, William, of Lincoln Coll.; B.A. 20 Oct., 1526, M.A. 11 Nov., 1529, one of these names fellow of Eton College 1538.
Norfolk, John (Northfolke), B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 3 July, 1536, M.A. 2 May, 1539, fellow Eton College 26 Feb., 1546, rector of Titchwell, Norfolk, 1546. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Maydwell, James (Madwell), fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1555, B.A. 17 June, 1555.
Chippingdale, John, B.A. 6 July, 1565 (? fellow Lincoln Coll. 1566), M.A.; B.Med. 1571, D.Med. (sup.) 16 July, 1572.
Harris, William, B.A. 26 Jan., 1564-5, fellow Lincoln Coll. 1567, M.A. 4 July, 1570, bursar 1571, studied theology at Douay; died 1602. See Ath. i. 724; & D.N.B.

Harte, William, (of Wells diocese), Traps scholar of Lincoln Coll. 25 May, 1571, supl. B.A. 18 June, 1574, hanged, drawn, and quartered for being a Romish priest, at York, 15 March, 1582. See Ath. i. 490.
Pottes, Peter, B.A. 18 Nov., 1569; fellow Lincoln Coll. 1571-8, M.A. 31 July, 1573, vicar of Milton 1577, and rector of Tunstall, Kent, 1580. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bunche, Thomas, Bible clerk of Lincoln Coll. 23 May, 1571; perhaps B.A. from Balliol Coll. 12 June, 1573, exhibitioner 1574.
Parkinson, Robert (Perkinson), B.A. 24 March, 1563-4, B.C.L. 18 Feb., 1569-70, M.A. 4 July, 1570; fellow Lincoln Coll. 1571.
Pitts, Walter, fellow Lincoln Coll. 1571.
Betham, Ralph, fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1573-7; B.A. 29 Jan., 1570-1, M.A. 3 July, 1576, one of these names vicar of Nafferton, Yorks, 1588. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., xii. 62.
Paule, John, B.A. 9 Nov., 1572; fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1573-86, M.A. 6 July, 1577, perhaps vicar of South Brent, Somerset, 1587. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Harwood, William (Harwode), B.A. 1 Dec. 1571, M.A. 3 July, 1576; fellow of Lincoln Coll. before 1573; died 1 May, 1578. See O.H.S. xii. 10.
Markham, William, B.A. sup. 28 March, 1571, fellow of Lincoln Coll. 1573-4; perhaps rector of Oving, Bucks, 1575. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Warner, Bartholomew (Warnar), of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19, "Dialecticus;" B.A. from St. John's Coll. 14 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 19 March, 1578-9, B.Med. 27 Jan., 1585-6, D.Med. 1 July, 1594, regius professor of medicine 1597-1612, and superior reader of Lynacre's lecture; buried 26 Jan., 1618-19, in the church of St. Mary Magdalen, Oxford, admon. at Oxford 29th. See Fasti, i. 267; & O.H.S. xi. 67n.

Printer, John, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 24; vicar of Worle, Somerset, 1582-98; and as Printoste alias Printer, he was rector of Stanton Prior 1572-82, rector of Dunkerton 1572-92, and vicar of Locking, 1579 (all Somerset), until his death in 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Smithe, Henry, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 15, B.A. 16 Feb., 1578-9; M.A. from St. John's Coll. 3 May, 1583; "silver tongued Smith" (s. Erasmus, of Bosworth, co. Leicester), lecturer of St. Clement's Danes 1587-8; died about 1592. See Ath. i. 603; & Fasti, i. 223.

Cave, (Sir) Thomas, of Northants. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 13; eldest son of Roger Cave, of Stanford, and knighted 9 July, 1603, died 6 Sept., 1613, aged 51, marble monument in Stanford church, father of Thomas 1608, and of Oliver 1610, and of Richard last named.

Mintorne, John, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18; "Mro. Tatam tut. gram.," student of Middle Temple 1578, as son and heir of John, of Batcombe, Dorset, gent.; brother of Henry 1582. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Opinshawe, Robert (or Orpinshawe), of Suffolk, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20; "Mro. Michell tut. dial.," B.A. sup. 4 May, 1574, rector of Benacre 1575, and of Wrentham, Suffolk, 1579. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Gre(e)ne, Michael, of Herts, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20, "dialecticus" described as "ex aedibus Daniell," B.A. 24 May, 1576, M.A. 19 March, 1578-9; for one of these names see N. & Q. 6 S. vol. 6, p. 285.

Codrington, Richard, of co. Gloucester. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 15; 'dialecticus,' of Lincoln's Inn 1580, perhaps M.P. Tavistock 1593. See also Francis same date & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Pocklington, William, of co. Lincoln, "serviens." Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19; "Grammaticus," B.A. 22 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 6 July, 1581; rector of Pickwell, co. Leicester, 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Codrington, Francis, of co. Gloucester. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 16; 'Dialecticus,' B.A. 23 Feb., 1578-9. See also Richard same date & Fosbrooke's History of Gloucestershire, ii. 23.

Benskin, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 19; ('Mro. Wilson tut. dial.') one of these names vicar of Ambrosden, Oxon, 1586. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Burdet, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 19; B.A. 8 July, 1578, rector of Burton Overy, co. Leicester, 1581. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Skeffington, William, of co. Stafford, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 16; created a baronet 8 May, 1627 (son of John, of Fisherwick, co. Stafford); died 16 Sept., 1635.
Greenwood, Richard (Grenewood), of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 16; "Dno. Michell tut gram.," B.A. 17 May, 1577, M.A. 1 July, 1581, rector of Playden, Sussex, 1593.
Clarke, William, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 19; B.A. 17 Feb., 1578-9, M.A. 1 July, 1581, one of these names fellow of Eton College 1599.
Williams, William, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18, "Mro. Michel tut. dial." perhaps B.A. 11 Feb., 1576-7, and B.D. 27 March, 1601, after 20 years in theology.
Hawkins, Robert (Haukin), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 21; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 7 July, 1578, M.A. 3 July, 1582, as " Hawkins."
Lane, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18; "Mro. Mitchell tut. gram."; B.A. 13 Feb., 1576-7, fellow 1577-89, M.A. 13 May, 1580.
Redshawe, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575 ("serviens Mri. Michell"), B.A. 21 Feb., 1576-7; will at Oxford proved 8 Dec., 1623.
Smith, Barnard, (senr.) of co. Lancaster, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 25, "Mro. Michell tut. gram."; perhaps B.A. 25 Feb., 1574-5.
Berrie, Henry, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 20; 'dialecticus,' described as 'ex ædibus Daniell.' See O.H.S., xi. 66.
Johnes, Francis, of co. Leic., gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 19; B.A. 12 June, 1578, fellow 1579-83.
Knolles, William, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18; "Mro. Potts tut. dial."; B.A. 25 Feb., 1577-8.
Cresson, William (Creson or Croson), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 22; 'Mro. Potts tut gram.'
Rippar, Robert, of co. Lincoln, "pleb. et serviens Mro. Gibson." Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 22, "Grammaticus."
Johnes, Anthony (Johannes), of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 15; "Dno. Hartley tut. dial."
Helaughe, William (Helaghe), of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19; "Dno. Hartley tut. dial."
Howet, Christopher, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 16; "Mro. Potts tut. gram."
Joyner, William, of co. Glouc., pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 17; "Mro. Mitchell, tut. gram."
Fre(e)man, Robert, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20; "Mro. Potts tut. Gram."
Smith, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19; "Dno. Weston tut. dial."
Witherstone, Thomas, of co. Worcester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 15, "Mro. Tatam, tut."
Morris, Philip, of co. Hereford, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 14; "Mro. Potts tut. gram."
Swetnam, Thomas, of co. Derby, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 19, "Dno. Harward tut. dial."
Willies, William, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 20, "Dno. Harwood tut. gram."
Philbye, William, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 15; "Dno. M. Horwood tut. gram."
Otes, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 21; "Mro. Holt, Oriel, tut. dial."
Clarke, Nicholas, of Oxon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 1575, aged 17; 'Mro Michell tut. gram.'
Mayne, Anthony, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 14; "Dno. Fulbecke tut. gram."
Raynar, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 15; "Mro. Michell tut. gram."
Shepparde, Walter, of Somerset, "pleb. serv. colleg." Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6.
Horton, John, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 21. "Mro. Michell tut. dial."
Stumpe, Basil, of Wilts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 16, "Mro. Gibson tut. gram."
Stumpe, James, of Wilts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 18, "Mro. Gibson tut. gram."
Pottle, William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 15 March, 1575-6, aged 19.
Lye, Hugh (Lie), of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1575, aged 14; "grammaticus."

Kilby, Richard, of co. Leic., pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16; fellow 1577, B.A. 9 Dec., 1578, M.A. 2 July, 1582, B. & D.D. 7 July, 1596; rector of his college 1590, canon of Lincoln 1601, and regius professor of Hebrew 1610, until his death, buried in All Saints' church 7 Nov., 1620; demy Magdalen Coll. 1572-7; will and inventory at Oxford 3 Nov., 1620, See Ath. ii. 287; Bloxam, iv. 188; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Cholmondeley, Randolph or Randle (Cholmley), of Cheshire, 'eq. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 19; of Lincoln's Inn 1580, son of Sir Hugh, and nephew of Randle, recorder of London and queen's serjeant. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Shenton, John, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20; perhaps rector of Croft, co. Leicester, 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tov(e)y, Thomas (Tovie or Tove), B.A. 26 Jan., 1569-70, fellow Lincoln Coll. 1577-87, M.A. 22 June, 1579; vicar of Thatcham, Bucks, 1586. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hughes, James (Hugh or Hughley), of co. Lancaster, "serviens." Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 22.
Coverley, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 1577, aged 18. See Calverley, page 232.
Silverwood, John, of Yorks. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1577, aged 20, serviens, B.A. 2 March, 1580-1.
Andrews, Simon (Andros), of Northants, 'eq. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Durande, Richard, of co. Leicester, 'serviens.' Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20.
Johnson, Peter, of co. Linc., "serviens." Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 22.
Nayler, Edward, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Cracrofte, John, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.
Sale, John, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 1577, aged 18; B.A. 27 May, 1579.
Shenton, Randolph, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Longdon, William, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20.
Fleshar, John, of Yorkshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 20.
Cock, John, of Essex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.

Simcocks, Thomas (Symcocks), of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date "30" June, 1578, aged 18, B.A. "12" June, 1578; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1586, as son and heir of Christopher, of Butleigh, Somerset, esq. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Weston, Edward, of London, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 12 (son of William), D.D. university of Montreale in Italy, taught 10 years at Douay, canon of the collegiate church of St. Mary at Bruges in Flanders. See Ath. ii. 573.

Hodges, William, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1582, as only son of James, of Somerton, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Crouch, Richard, of Beds, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 23; sup. for B.A. 18 Feb., 1586-7, rector of Outwell, Norfotk, 1595. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Webbe, John, of Wilts, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16, B.A. supd. 2 July, 1582. See O.H.S. xii. 108.
Bentley, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 19; B.A. 29 March, 1582.
Johnes, Griffith, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 21.
Oswald, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16.
Grantham, Ralph, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 16.
Qua(r)termayn, Francis, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 12.
Chambers, William, of Notts, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 20 March, 1578-9, aged 18.
Bowker, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 30 June, 1578, aged 17.
Williams, Anthony, of Bucks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 30 June, 1578, aged 28.
Storye, John, of Oxon. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 30 June, 1578, aged 22, serviens.

Newbie, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 18; fellow 1582, B.A. 16 March, 1582-3, M.A. 7 July, 1585; died 6 Nov., 1587, inventory at Oxford 23 April, 1588. See O.H.S. xii. 111.

Lewen, Robert, of "Lacenstrensis" (co. Leicester). Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 18; B.A. 29 Nov., 1582, fellow 1583-7, M.A. 9 July, 1585; buried in Bath abbey 16 Sept., 1617.
Latner, Edward (? Latimer), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 18 (called Lootner by Chester).
Elmestone, John, of Kent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 22; B.A. 16 Feb., 1581-2, M.A. 9 June, 1585.
Dakin, Richard (Daken), of co. Leicester ('Lacestrensis'), pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 17.
Jacob, John, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 17 Dec., 1579, aged 18.

Boothe, (Sir) George, (s. William) of Cheshire, mil. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 14; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1584, knighted 1596, and created a baronet 22 May, 1611, married in 1577 when 11 years old, died 24 Oct., 1652, grandfather of George Lord Delamere, and brother of Edmund 1584. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Sutton, John, baronis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 12; of Sedgley Park, baptised at St. Edmund's, Dudley, 30 Nov., 1569; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 3 July, 1592 (as Dudley), perhaps M.P. Newtown 1593; buried at Sedgley 3 March, 1644-5, as "Mr. John Dudley"; brother of Edward Sutton same date.

Sutton, Edward, baronis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 14 (as Duddeley), 9th baron Dudley (son of Edward 8th baron), summoned to parliament from 19 Feb., 1593-4, to 3 Nov., 1639; died 23 June, 1643; brother of John 1580, and father of Ferdinando.

Bradshawe, Henry, of co. Radnor, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 19, and of Jesus Coll. 24 Nov., 1581; B.A. from Christ Church 5 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 16 March, 1586-7, admon. (at Oxford) granted 29 Feb., 1616.

Dudley, John (Duddeley), baronis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 12; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 3 July, 1592, son of Edward, 8th Baron Dudley, perhaps M.P. Newtown 1593, brother of Edward same date. See Sutton.
Dudley, Edward (Duddeley), baronis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 14; 9th Baron Dudley, brother of John same date. See Sutton.
Wayte, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 19; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 28 Feb., 1583-4.
Minchen, John (Mynchen), of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 17.
Burne, Roger, of co. Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 20.
Bradshawe, Thomas, of Radnor, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 22.
Carter, Nicholas, of Beds, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 17.
Redburne, Martin, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date 24 July, 1580, aged 14.

Oglethorpe, Robert, of Yorks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 17; bar.-at-law, of Gray's Inn, 1597 (as of Birdsea, Yorks), a baron of the exchequer, Ireland, 1605; knighted 6 Jan., 1608, by Sir Arthur Chichester, lord deputy. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Seaborne, William, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 16; B.A. 22 and 28 June, 1585, M.A. 21 Nov., 1589; rector of Thornton-in-the-Moors, Cheshire, 1590. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Walter, of co. Monmouth, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 15, "serviens" B.A. 24 Nov., 1589; perhaps vicar of Llantrissant, co. Monmouth, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Towse, Edward, of Suffolk, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 18, B.A. 10 July, 1585; fellow Exeter Coll. 1592-1600, M.A. 23 May, 1590, B.C.L. supd. 27 Oct., 1595. See Boase, 50.
Watson, James, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 22, B.A. 23 Feb., 1586-7; probably vicar of Ermington, Devon, 1587, and father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Willoughby, William, of co. Warwick, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1594. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Jennings, Marmaduke (Jeninges), of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 16; student of Lincoln's Inn 1585. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pyne, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1591. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Catchmay, Richard (Chatchmay), of co. Gloucester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 15; one of these names knighted 19 June, 1608.
Marshall, William, of co. Worcester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 18; B.A. 14 Dec., 1585, M.A. 7 June, 1592.
Fowler, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 22, "serviens;" B.A. 29 March, 1582.
Geste, Edward (Geyste), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 21; B.A. 28 June, 1585.
Withington, Richard, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 21, B.A. supd. 15 April, 1586.
Oglethorpe, Andrew (Ogelthroppe), of Yorks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 15.
Hampden, Thomas (Hamdenne), of Northants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 17.
Addison, Henry (Addeson), of Yorks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1581, aged 17.
Dimblebie, Robert, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric.-entry under date circa 1581, aged 11.
Gleave, Thomas, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 16.
Peache, Simon, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1581, aged 16.
Aldersey, William, of Cheshire, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Alleine, John, of co. Hereford, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Walker, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Nov., 1581, aged 18.

Baynham, John (Beynham), of co. Hereford, 'pleb. fil. cond. Gen.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1595, as son and heir of John, of Bromyard, co. Hereford, gent., one of these names M.P. Queenborough 1593. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Payne, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 17; B.A. 13 June, 1586, M.A. 3 Dec., 1589, rector of Stockerston, co. Leicester, 1593; see William same date. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Franklyn, Henry, of co. York, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1582, aged 15; possibly student of Middle Temple 1586, as son and heir of Richard, of Midridge, co. Durham, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Borrowes, Ismaell, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 27 April, 1582, aged 16; B.A. 29 Jan., 1585-6, M.A. 18 June, 1589, sometime minister of Stanton, co. Leicester, father of Thomas Borough 1631.
Irish, Freeman, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1582, aged 17 (subs. "Fremund le Irevis," but see O.H.S. xi. 122). See Visitation of Dorset for Le Iris or Ireys.
Lee, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1582-3, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1600, as of Maidstone. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Walker, Alexander, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 15 Feb., 1582-3, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1594. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
O'Brien, Thady (Thadeus Bryan), comitis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Sept., 1582, aged 15; probably Teige, 2nd son of Connor, or Cornelius, 3rd earl of Thomond.
Wheatley, William (Whitleaye), of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 May, 1582, aged 20; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 4 Feb., 1585-6, M.A. 6 July, 1588.
Smithe, Thomas (Smythe), of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1582-3, aged 16; perhaps student of Gray's Inn 1586. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Rotherham, John, of Beds, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 27 April, 1582, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1586. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Denson, William, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1582, aged 17; B.A. 27 April, 1586, M.A. 2 July, 1588.
Blinston, Peter, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1582, aged 17; B.A. (sup. 15 April) 1586.
Colthurst, Henry (Coulthurst), of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1582-3, aged 17.
Morgan, Roger, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1582-3, aged 20.
Clutterbuck, Richard (Clutterbooke), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1583, aged 16; B.A. 23 Feb., 1586-7, perhaps M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 16 April, 1594, as Robert.
Manby, W. (Manbee), of co. Lincoln, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry dated 31 May, 1583, aged 11; probably William, son of Francis Manby, of Elsham, co. Lincoln.

Reade, Henry, of Hants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 30 Oct. 1584, aged 18; B.A. 13 June, 1588, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1596 (as son and heir of Andrew, of Faccombe, Hants, gent.); possibly M.P. Andover 1588-9. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Lewes, John, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 14; B.A. 21 Nov., 1589, M.A. 7 July, 1592, vicar of Cutcombe, Somerset, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Marriot, Richard, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 13; B.A. 11 July, 1590, M.A. 10 May, 1594.
Rotherham, Thomas, of Beds, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1584, aged 16; B.A. 14 June, 1588, M.A. 17 May, 1591.
Kinde, Myles (Kynde), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 20; B.A. 23 Feb., 1586-7.
Grant, Samuel (Graunt), of co. Worcester, pleb, Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 17.
Gwillim, John (Gyllyam), of Northants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 March 1584-5, aged 54.
Grove, Francis, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1584, aged 15.

Debancke, John (Deabancke), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 20; B.A. from Merton Coll. 15 July, 1590, rector of Bradwell-juxta-Mare 1564, until his death in 1602. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; for three licences to marry see Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, p. 392.
Pawsonn, Richard, of Hants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1585, aged 19 (? Dawson).
Orson, Nathaniel, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 2 July, 1585, aged 13.

Woode, William, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 June, 1586, aged 20; perhaps B.A. from University Coll. 23 Oct., 1590; and rector of Great Houghton, Northants, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Holden, Alexander, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; B.A. 26 Feb., 1589-90, vicar of Bardsey, Yorks, 1597. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Swetnam, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18, B.A. 4 Feb., 1591-2.
Wilcox, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 19.
Buttell, William, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1586, aged 48.
Herdman, Gabriel, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 April, 1586, aged 18.
Fisher, Matthew, of Berks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 27 Oct., 1586, aged 32.
Walker, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 21.
Beard, George, of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 17.

Burbage, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 May, 1587, aged 15; B.A. 15 Dec., 1592, M.A. 3 April, 1596, B.D. 18 Jan., 1604-5, licenced to preach 19 Nov., 1605, rector of Porlock 1611, until his death in 1617, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Balme, Robert (Bawlme), of London, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1587, aged 17; B.A. 10 Feb., 1590-1, M.A. 17 May, 1595, vicar of Staunton Drew, 1592, and rector of Butcombe, Somerset, 1609, and of Llangattock, co. Mon., 1610. See Foster's Index.
Nedham, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1587, aged 14. See next entry.
Marshe, Richard, of London (city), pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1587-8, aged 14.
Brett, John, of co. Huntingdon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 May, 1587, aged 17.
Heardman, Henry, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1587, aged 19.

Herberte, Matthew, of co. Montgomery, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 March, 1588-9, aged 26; student of the Inner Temple 1583, as of Dolegeog, co. Montgomery (2s. Edward), served in the Low Countries, M.P. Montgomery 1586-7. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Burbage, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1588-9, aged 18; B.A. 15 Dec., 1592, M.A. 6 July, 1598. See O.H.S., xii. 173.
Lodington, Marmaduke, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 April, 1588, aged 14; B.A. 24 April, 1592, M.A. sup. 10 Feb., 1594-5.
Brombye, William, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Aug., 1588, aged 17; (? B.A. 1595, as Matthew).
Wartnabye, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 April, 1588, aged 18.
Howkins, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 April, 1588, aged 18.
Reade, William, of Hants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 March, 1588, aged 15.

Parker, Robert, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 May, 1589, aged 16; B.A. 3 July, 1592, M.A. 31 Jan., 1595-6, then in orders, rector of Ashton-under-Lyne, co. Lancaster, 1605. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Berkeley, Thomas (Barkeley), of Devon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 July, 1589, aged 15; a student of the Inner Temple 1591, as of Okenbury, Devon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fre(e)man, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1589, aged 15; B.A. 25 June, 1593, M.A. 6 July, 1598.
Watts, Samuel, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 30 Jan., 1589-90, aged 18, B.A. 19 June, 1593, M.A. 2 July, 1596.
Allen, Thomas (Allin), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 May, 1589, aged 14; B.A. 3 Feb., 1592-3.
Hurrell, Thomas, of Devon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 July, 1589, aged 16; B.A. 3 Feb., 1592-3.
Pickard, Laurence, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 20; B.A. 12 Dec., 1593, M.A. 6 July, 1598, rector of West Parley, Dorset, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Donning, Richard (Donnyng), of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 18; B.A. 30 June, 1593, M.A. 4 July, 1600, as Downing.
Dive, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 17; B.A. 26 Feb., 1594-5. M.A. 20 Oct., 1597.
Hartley, Anthony, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 22; B.A. 4 Dec., 1592, M.A. 1 July, 1600.
Churchey, George, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1590-1, aged 18.
Elmestone, Edward, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Jan. 1590-1, aged 20.

Ireland, Thomas, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Feb., 1591-2, aged 14; B.A. 26 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 20 Oct., 1597, B.D. 13 June, 1605, D.D. 10 April, 1611, student of Middle Temple 1597 (as 3rd son of John, citizen and salter of London), rector of Stoke Hammond, Bucks, 1604, canon of York 1608, and of Southwell 1612, rector of Barton-in-Fabis, Notts, 1612, etc. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Index Eccl.

Lloyd, John, of co. Cardigan, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 26 Feb., 1594-5; one of these names vicar of Llandrillo, co. Merioneth, 1604, treasurer of Bangor Cathedral with rectory of Llanvihangle-y-Traith, co. Merioneth, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Harrington, William, of co. Lancaster, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 15; perhaps 2nd son of Percival, of Huyton, co. Lancaster.
Debancke, Hugh, of co. Leicester, 'cler. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Oct., 1591, aged 18; B.A. 26 Feb., 1594-5. See O.H.S., xii. 188.
Forsett, Edward, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1591, aged 19. See Ath. ii. 5.
Webb, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18, B.A. 8 July, 1595.
Pendleton, Edward, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1591, aged 19.
Cracroft, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1591, aged 18.
Fitch, Robert, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1591, aged 15.
Acranley, William (Accranley), of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 2 March, 1592-3, aged 18; B.A. 20 Jan., 1595-6.
Hunt, John, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 19; B.A. 26 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 6 July, 1598.
Grover, William, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1592, aged 18.
Hill, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1592, aged 18.
Read, Richard, of Beds, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 March, 1592-3, aged 30.

Hastings, (Sir) Henry, of co. Leicester. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 May, 1593, aged 15 (as arm.); student of Gray's Inn 1595 (as son and heir of Walter, of Kirkby, co. Leicester, esq.), of Kirby and Bramston, co. Leicester; knighted 23 April, 1603; perhaps M. P. Leicestershire 1601, 1604-11, 1624-5, 1626; died 15 Sept., 1649. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Turvill, (Sir) Ambrose, of Herts, arm. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 25 May, 1593, aged 12; student of Middle Temple 1597 (as son and heir of Geoffrey, of London, esq., deceased), knighted 11 May, 1603, M.P. Minehead 1604-11. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Price, Richard, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 14 May, 1596.
Rutherforth, Robert (Rotherforth), of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 14; B.A. 21 Feb., 1596-7.
Rutherforth, John (Rotherforth), of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 15; B.A. 21 Feb., 1596-7.
Hartley, Edward, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1593-4, aged 15; B.A. 15 April, 1597.
Lloyd, Gruffith, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18.

Veale, Walter, of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 19, B.A. 20 Jan., 1595-6, M.A. 10 June, 1607, then in orders; vicar of Winkleigh 1603, and rector of Iddesleigh, Devon, 1616; father of the next, of Richard, and of Samuel 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hippesley, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14; B.A. 21 June, 1598; student of Middle Temple 1599 (as 3rd son of John, of Ston Easton, Somerset, esq.; brother of Richard 1594. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Mayne, Simon, of Herts, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17; B.A. 4 July, 1597, M.A. 1 July, 1600; of Dinton, Bucks (2s. Henry, of Bovendon); died 1617, aged 40; father of Simon the regicide; brother of John 1598.

Morton, John, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 17; B.A. 21 June, 1598, fellow, M.A. 17 May, 1601 (incorporated at Cambridge 1609), B.D. 2 July, 1610; admon. at Oxford 2 Nov., 1614.

Moore, Richard, of co. Lancaster, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 15; B.A. 9 Feb., 1597-8; student of Gray's Inn 1595, as son of William, of Bank House, co. Lancaster. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Stre(e)te, Nicholas, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1596, as son and heir of Nicholas, of Taunton, Somerset, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Northam, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17; B.A. sup. 24 May, 1598, M.A. 9 July, 1601, perhaps vicar of Rowston, co. Lincoln; father of James and William.
Gaye, William, of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 July, 1594, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1595, as son and heir of John, of Chilley, Devon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Mollineux, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1595, as of (West) Derby, co. Lancaster. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Fewkes, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 19 (as Fowkes); B.A. 12 Nov., 1597.
Grantham, William, of co. Lincoln, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 July, 1594, aged 17; perhaps brother of Thomas 1602.
Hippesley, Richard, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14; brother of Thomas same date.
Milward, John (Myllward), of co. Derby, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 19.
Powell, Griffith, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 22.
Sims, Christopher, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14.
Burton, Thomas, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17.
Astley, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 18.
Lloyd, William, of co. Cardigan, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 14.
Lloyd, Rees, of co. Cardigan, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1594, aged 17.
Lloyd, Walter, of co. Cardigan, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1595, aged 15.

Farwell, George, of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17; B.A. 21 June, 1598, student of Middle Temple 1597 (as 5s. of John, of Holbrooke, Somerset, esq.), perhaps knighted 20 Jan., 1610; died May, 1648. See N. & Q. 5S. iv. 413-4; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Cowarde, Edward, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 15; B.A. 24 Jan., 1599-1600, fellow, M.A. 10 June, 1602, B.D. 31 May, 1609, rector of Porlock, Somerset, 1610, admon. (at Oxford) granted 18 March, 1611. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Pridgeon, Philip, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1596, aged 16; B.A. 31 Oct., 1600, M.A. 6 Dec., 1604, rector of Withern, co. Lincoln, 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Barrodall, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 17; B.A. 10 July, 1600, M.A. 9 July, 1612.
W(h)arton, Anthony, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 5 Nov., 1596, aged 13, B.A. 12 Feb., 1601-2.
Perry, Edward (Perye), of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 19.

Anyan, Thomas, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. — Nov., 1597, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 7 June, 1602, M.A. 2 May, 1606, B.D. 10 Dec., 1612, D.D. 6 July, 1614, president of Corpus Christi College 1614-29, rector of Beckenham, Kent, and Ashstead, Surrey, 1613, preb. of Gloucester 1612, and of Canterbury 1614 (Le Neve), rector of Checkendon, Oxon, 1625, and of Cranley, Surrey, 1629, chaplain to the king, died at Canterbury 1632. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, i. 359.

Chapman, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 June, 1597, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1611 (as of Foxton, co. Leicester, gent.), treasurer 1646-8, appointed serjt.-at-law 1648, but declared illegal on the restoration 1660, mayor of Lincoln 1607, and recorder 1624-52, son of Edward, of Middleton Manor, co. Leicester. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Sherarde, (Sir) Philip (Sheararde), of co. Leicester, arm. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry — Dec., 1597, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1599 (1s. Francis, of Stapleford, co. Leicester); knighted 23 April, 1603; died 23 April, 1624, brother of George and William 1602. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Lutterell, Thomas, of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 15; B.A. 3 July, 1599, M.A. 8 July, 1602; of Dunster Castle (s. George), student of Lincoln's Inn 1601, M.P. Minehead 1625; buried 7 Feb., 1643-4; brother of George and John. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Woodward, Tristram, of Middx., gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry — July, 1597, aged 12; student of Middle Temple 1601, son and heir of William, of St. Andrew's, Holborn, Middlesex, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Browne, Abraham, of Hants, 'cler. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 June, 1597, aged 16; a student of the Middle Temple 1598, as son and heir of Abraham, of Winchester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Smithe, Barnabas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. — Dec., 1597, aged 15, B.A. 17 Dec., 1601, M.A. 7 July, 1604; rector of North Witham, co. Lincoln, 1610. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Sherarde, John, of co. Lincoln, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry Dec., 1597, aged 13; student of Gray's Inn 1604, as son of William, of Lobthorpe, co. Lincoln, kt. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Jaspar, Humphrey, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 21; one of these names vicar of South Cerney, co. Gloucester, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mayne, John, of Herts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1597-8, aged 14; brother of Simon 1594.
Anderson, Edward, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. Nov., 1597, aged 16.

Stringer, Walter, of co. Worcester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1598, aged 17, B.A. 12 Feb., 1601-2; of Chelsea, Middlesex, licenced 17 Dec., 1614, to marry Katherine, daughter of Emanuel Trumball. See London Marriage Liceences, ed. Foster.
Hastings, Robert, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1598, aged 16; perhaps rector of East Keal (north), co. Lincoln 1613, and of the south part 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chippingdale, John, of co. Leicester, D.C.L. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 June, 1598, aged 14; probably same as next entry, and brother of Tobias.
Reade, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Dec., 1598, aged 19; B.A. 10 June, 1602, M.A. 24 April, 1605, B.D. 6 July, 1614.
Lea, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 17 Feb., 1603-4.
Dilke, Nathaniel, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 June, 1598, aged 16.

Nevill, Gervis, of co. Lincoln, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 9 July, 1603, M.A. 1 April, 1607, B.D. 6 July, 1614 (s. George, of Ragnell, and of Grove, Notts, by his wife, Mary Williamson), rector of Grove 1611, and of Headon, Notts, 1631, vicar of Roystone, Notts, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Heane, Thomas (Heyne), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17; B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5, M.A. 9 July, 1612 (s. Robert, of Thurcaston or Thrussington, co. Leicester), usher of Merchant Taylors' school; died 27 July, 1645; buried in Christ Church, Newgate. See Ath. iii. 173; & D.N.B.

Cooper, John, of Sussex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 19; query B.A. from New Coll. 6 Dec., 1604, M.A. 6 June, 1607, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1610, as son and heir of Thomas, of Arundel, Sussex, gent.; father of William 1632. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wyndham, Henry (Windham), of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 May, 1599, aged 18; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 3 July, 1601, student of Lincoln's Inn 1603, as son and heir of John, of Somerset, a master of the bench. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stradlinge, John, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 May, 1599, aged 20; student of Lincoln's Inn 1600, being kinsman to Sir John Popham, lord chief justice of England. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Tookye, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. 9 May, 1603, M.A. 28 Jan., 1605-6; rector of Galby, co. Leicester, 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bonnytt, John (Bonnett), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 15; B.A. from Merton Coll. 17 Feb., 1603-4, M.A. from St. Alban Hall 15 Dec., 1606.
Herbert, Humfrey, of co. Montgomery, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 17; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 15 Feb., 1603-4, M.A. 11 July, 1607.
Vaughan, Roger, of co. Montgomery, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 March, 1599-1600, aged 15; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 16 Feb., 1603-4.
Sutton, Gilbert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. 2 July, 1603, M.A. 2 July, 1606.
Blower, Robert, of Oxon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 April, 1599, aged 15; B.A. 10 June, 1602, M.A. 24 April, 1605.
Phillips, George, of Salop, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 April, 1599, aged 15.
Ayshford, Robert, of Devon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1599, aged 19.

Robinson, Josias, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 17 Oct., 1600, aged 18; B.A. 30 June, 1604, M.A. 22 April, 1607; incorporated at Cambridge 1609, rector of Elston, Notts, 1612, and Kirk Deighton, Yorks, 1637; father of Thomas 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Burton, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 15; B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5; M.A. 2 July, 1608. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Lloyd, George, of co. Cardigan, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1600, aged 15.

Richardson, William, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 14, B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5; student of Middle Temple 1606, as son and heir of Thomas, of Sandwich, Kent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Plummer, Nicholas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 19; B.A. 5 July, 1605, M.A. 7 July, 1608; rector of Southampton All Saints 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Nicholes, Roger, of Bucks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1614, as of Willen, Bucks. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Fisher, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17; B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5; student of Lincoln's Inn 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hunston, William, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15. See Herald's Visitation.
Thimble, John, of Essex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 21, B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5.
Wolston, James (Woolston), of Devon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 21.
Watson, William, of co. Worcester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 15.

Odber, Robert, of Southants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 March, 1602, aged 15, B.A. 13 June, 1605; student of Middle Temple 1605, as son and heir of Robert, of Hurne, Hants, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Toone, Thurstan, of Cheshire, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1602, aged 18 (subscribes "Therstans"), B.A. 2 July, 1606, M.A. 1 June, 1609; rector of Collingtree, Northants, 1630. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Grantham, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 May, 1602, aged 15; perhaps brother of William 1594, and sons of Thomas, of Langton by Wragby, co. Lincoln.
Balden, John, of co. Southampton, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 March, 1602, aged 137; B.A. 28 Feb., 1605-6, M.A. 7 July, 1614 (as Baldwin).
Frye, Henry, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 March, 1602, aged 18; B.A. 28 Feb., 1605-6.
Bayley, John (Bailey), of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 Dec., 1602, aged 14.
Willarde, William, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 July, 1602, aged 17.

Sanderson, Robert, of Yorks, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15; B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5, fellow 1606-19, M.A. 20 Oct., 1607 (incorporated at Cambridge 1609), proctor 1616, B.D. 19 May, 1617, licenced to preach 1 July, 1617, created D.D. 31 Aug., 1636, canon of Christ Church 1642, regius professor of divinity 1642-8, 1660-1; (s. Robert), born at Sheffield, Yorks, 19 Sept., 1587, baptised next day, rector of Wyberton 1618-19, vicar of Heckington 1618, and rector of Boothby Paynell, (all) co. Lincoln, 1619, chaplain to Dr. Mounttain, bishop of Lincoln 1619; canon of Lincoln 1629, and of Southwell 1641, chaplain in ordinary to the king Nov., 1631, rector of Muston, co. Leicester, 1633, bishop of Lincoln 1660, until his death 29 Jan., 1662, buried in the chancel of Bugden church, father of Thomas 1639. See Ath. iii. 623; Burrows, 490, 507; Lansdowne MS. 986, f. 37; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Chippingdale, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18; B.A. 28 Feb., 1605-6, M.A. 7 July, 1608, see preceding entry.
Vere, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 1 July, 1603, aged 13; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 6 July, 1610.
Selleck, John (Sellak), of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 June, 1603, aged 20.
Watkins, William, of co. Hereford, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18.
Holden, James, of Essex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 June, 1603, aged 18.

Reade, Michael, of co. Leicester pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 16; B.A. 20 Oct., 1608, M.A. 12 June, 1611 (incorporated at Cambridge 1633); created D.D. 31 Aug., 1636, rector of Little Gidding, Hunts, 1614, and of Waddesden (3rd portion), Bucks, 1619, rector of Cottesford, Oxon, 1625-47, and of Polebrook, Northants, 1639, father of John 1638. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 21.

Scampton, Matthew, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 15; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 17 Feb., 1608-9; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 6 July, 1613, rector of Hoby, co. Leicester, 1617, father of the next; one William Scampton, rector of Stony Stanton, co. Leicester, 1592, possibly father of Matthew. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hopton, (Sir) Arthur, of Somerset, K.B., fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 15, B.A. 20 Oct., 1607, student of Middle Temple 1609 (as 3rd son of Arthur, of Witham, Somerset, kt.), knighted 2 Feb., 1637-8, ambassador in Spain; died 6 March, 1649-50, aged 62. See Ath. ii. 151; Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; & D.N.B.

Kilbie, Richard, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 17; B.A. 20 Oct., 1608, M.A. 6 July, 1613, B.D. 9 April, 1622; canon of Lincoln 1627, perhaps rector of Loddington, Northants, 1641, until sequestered 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MSS. 15,669-71.

Lodington, Robert, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 June, 1604, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 13 Feb., 1606-7; fellow of Brasenose Coll., M.A. 6 Feb., 1609-10, principal of New Inn Hall 1621-6, rector of Hotham, Yorks, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Orme, John, of co. Lincoln, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 June, 1604, aged 16; B.A. 15 March, 1607-8; perhaps vicar of Greetham 1616, and of Exton, Rutland, 1617. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Sutton, Fardinando, 1s. (Edward), Baron (Dudley), Lincoln Coll., matric. 27 Nov., 1604, aged 15; knight of the bath at the creation of Henry, Prince of Wales, in 1610; died 22 Nov., 1621.
Hawker, Silas, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 June, 1604, aged 16; B.A. 9 July, 1607, M.A. 2 July, 1610, vicar of Littlebourne, Kent, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Tookye, Job, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 June, 1604, aged 20, B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5, M.A. 20 Oct., 1607; incorporated at Cambridge 1612.
Marriett, William, of Northants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 June, 1604, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1611. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Lea, William, of Southants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 18; B.A. 15 July, 1608, M.A. 22 May, 1611.
Browne, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 16.
Williams, William, of co. Glamorgan, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 18 May, 1604, aged 15.
Clayton, Ralph, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 16.

Henley, Robert, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1605, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1616, bencher 1645 (as son and heir of Andrew, of Taunton, Somerset, gent.); one of the six clerks in chancery 1618-32, chief clerk or master in King's bench, an alderman of the city of London; buried in the Temple church 29 Feb., 1655-6; father of Andrew 1639. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Upton, John, of Devon, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 15, B.A. 9 June, 1608; student of Inner Temple 1610, as of Lupton, Devon, gent. (1s. Arthur, of Brixham), M.P. Clifton-Dartmouth-Hardness 1625, 1626, 1628-9, April-May 1640, and 1640 (L.P.), till his death 11 Sept., 1641. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Warde, John, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 19, B.A. 17 Nov., 1608.
Goven, Henry, of Southants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 19; B.A. 23 June, 1609.
Meyrick, Rice (Mirick), of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 July, 1605, aged 13.
Gwynn, John, of co. Glamorgan, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1605, aged 19.

Evans, Richard, of co. Montgomery, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1606, aged 18; B.A. from Hart Hall 20 Feb., 1611-12, M.A. 4 May, 1619 (then in orders), perhaps vicar of Llanrwst, co. Denbigh, 1618, vicar of Tremeirchion, co. Flint, 1620, vicar of Welshpool, co. Montgomery, 1622, rector of Halkin, Flints, 1626, and of Llanasa, Flints, 1633; deprived by the Puritan approvers; father of Richard 1663. See Thomas, 255; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Read, Thomas, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1606, aged 15; B.A. 30 Jan., 1609-10, M.A. 6 July, 1613, B.D. 9 April, 1622; perhaps sequestered from vicarage of Offley 1645, and rector of Ayott St. Lawrence, Herts, 1646. See Add. MSS. 15,670-71.

Barradall, Thomas, of co. Leicester, 'cler. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 15; B.A. 30 Jan., 1609-10, M.A. 6 July, 1613; one of these names rector of East Farndon, Northants, 1622, See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Brooksbey, Matthew (Brookesbye), of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 6 March, 1606-7, aged 15; B.A. 25 Oct., 1610, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1616, as of Ashby Magna, co. Leic., gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Robinson, Ralph, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 16; B.A. 13 Feb., 1609-10, M.A. 6 July, 1613; perhaps rector of Boultham, co. Lincoln, 1617. See Fasti, i. 334; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Parker, James, of Cornwall, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 6 March, 1606-7, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1610, as son and heir of William, of Bliston, Cornwall. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Streatinge, John, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 17, B.A. 14 Dec., 1614, M.A. 7 July, 1617, as Streeting.
Bunninge, Anthony, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 17; B.A. 30 Jan., 1609-10, M.A. 6 July, 1613.
Underhill, William, of co. Stafford, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 6 March, 1606-7, aged 18, B.A. 5 May, 1609.
Moseley, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1606, aged 17.

Manwood, Roger, "Militus de Balneo. fil." Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 15; B.A. 25 Jan., 1609-10, student of Inner Temple 1610, as son and heir of Sir Peter, of St. Stephen's, Canterbury, etc., aforesaid; brother of Charles 1615, and Thomas 1611. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Edlin, Michael, of co. Southampton, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1607, aged 19; B.A. 4 July, 1611, M.A. 7 July, 1614, vicar of Ham, Wilts, 1618; another of these names vicar of Dodderhill, co. Worc., 1572, and rector of East Woodhay, Hants, 1578. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wilkins, Roger, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1607, aged 19, B.A. 30 Jan., 1609-10; (s. Thomas, rector of Porthkerry and St. Marychurch, co. Glamorgan) rector of St. Marychurch; died 26 Dec., 1648; father of the next.
Sandys, William, of Bucks, militis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 July, 1607, aged 16; perhaps "Golden Sandys," eldest son of Sir Edwin, of Latimers, Bucks. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.
Bent, Nicholas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1607, aged 19; B.A. 13 Feb., 1611-12, rector of Braybrooke, Northants, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Iles, John, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 14; B.C.L. from Hart Hall 19 Jan., 1624-5, a student of Gray's Inn 1610. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Phayre, Luke (Phaire), of Yorks, sacerd. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1607, aged 17.

Baber, John, of Somerset, s.t.p. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 April, 1608, aged 15; B.A. 22 May, 1611 (son and heir of John, of Tormarton, co. Gloucester. D.D.), bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1621, bencher 1639, recorder of Wells and M.P. 1628-9, and April to May, 1640, died in 1644, aged 50. See Foster's Dictionary of M.P.'s.

Menes, (Sir) Matthew, of Kent, gen. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 April, 1608, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1610 (as Mennes, of Sandwich, Kent), knight of the bath at the coronation of Charles I.; brother of John last-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Ath. iii. 926.

Staresmore, John, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1608-9, aged 19, B.A. 30 June, 1612, M.A. 27 April, 1615; vicar of Coventry Holy Trinity 1618, rector of Great Warley, Essex, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Whorwood, John, of co. Stafford, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1608-9, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1612, as son and heir of Gerrard, of Stourton Castle, co. Stafford, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Johnson, Ambrose, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1608, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1611, as son and heir of Thomas, of St. Dunstan's-in-the-West, London. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Luttrell, George, of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1608, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1614, as son of George, of Dunster, Somerset; brother of John next-named. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Luttrell, John, of Somerset, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1608, aged 17; brother of George and Thomas 1597.
Lythall, Thomas, of co. Stafford, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1608-9, aged 19.
Wathen, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 15 April, 1608, aged 16.

Levete, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 16; B.A. 8 Feb., 1612-3, B.C.L. of Orleans University; incorporated 11 Feb., 1625-6; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1621, as son and heir of Thomas, of Melton-onthe-Hill, Yorks, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Fasti, i. 424.
Harris, John, of Devon, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 18, called Norys in Mat. Reg. John Norrys graduated B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 5 July, 1615. See also O.H.S. xi. 318-19.
Davies, Robert, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 17; B.A. 3 March, 1613-14, M.A. 4 July, 1616.
West, Matthew, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 15, B.A. 31 Jan., 1615-16, M.A. 4 Nov., 1618.
Barker, Edward, of co. Derby, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 18.
Hallett, John, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 June, 1610, aged 17.

Phillipps, Ambrose, of Salop, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 May, 1611, aged 17; B.A. 15 Feb., 1614-15, M.A. 17 June, 1619, vicar of Eaton 1617, and of Westbury (dexter part), Salop, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Quarme, Walter, of Devon, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1612, aged 15; B.A. 31 Jan., 1615-16, M.A. 1 July, 1618 (5s. Robert, of Woodhouse, Devon, after of St. Keverne, Cornwall), rector of Mawnam, Cornwall, 1622, until his death 27 Aug., 1662; buried there shortly after he was ejected; father of John 1659. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Calamy, i. 355.
Harrison, Richard, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1612, aged 16; B.A. 31 Jan., 1615-16, vicar of Heckington, co. Lincoln, 1619. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Palmer, George, of Rutland, pleb. Lincoln Coll. matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 18, B.A. 21 June, 1615, M.A. from Magdalen Coll. 10 July, 1618; B.D. from Exeter Coll. 14 Dec., 1626, fellow Lincoln Coll. 1621-31; supd. 17 Dec., 1630, for licence to preach; rector of St. George, Southwark, 1631, of St. Gabriel Fenchurch, London, 1632, of Farnham Royal, Bucks, 1637, until sequestered 1643 by the Westminster assembly of divines, vicar of Northall, Middlesex, 1638. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 91.

Evans, Matthew, of co. Montgomery, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 11 June, 1616, M.A. 24 April, 1619, (son of John, rector of Penegoes 1588-1632), rector of Llanddulas, co. Denbigh, 1616-20, vicar of Machynlleth 1616, rector of Llanmeriwig, co. Montgomery, 1620, rector of Penegoes, co. Montgomery, 1635, sequestrated 1646; father of John 1647. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Thomas, 336; & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 46.

N(o)urse, Thomas, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 15; B.A. 1 July, 1618, M.A. 19 April, 1621, licenced to practice medicine 20 Nov., 1636, D.Med. 3 July, 1641, of great practice in the city of Westminster, a licentiate of the college of physicians 1641; died 9 June, 1667. See Munk's Roll, i. 230; Chester's West. Abbey Reg. 165; & Fasti, ii. 4.

Hampsonn, Thomas, of co. Gloucester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1615-16, aged 18; a student of Middle Temple 1626 (as son and heir of Francis, of Broadwell, co. Gloucester, gent.), licenced 11 May, 1627, to marry Martha, daughter of Anthony Palmer, esq., of St. Dunstan's-in-theWest. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Cornewall, (Sir) Gilbert, of Salop, 'baronis fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 15; B.A. 21 March, 1616-17, as eq. aur. fil. (son of Sir Thomas Cornewall, knt., baron of Burford, Salop, named below), knighted at Theobalds, Dec., 1620, M.P. Bishop Castle 1621-2, buried in the chancel of Burford 5 Oct., 1671, grandfather of the next.

Manwood, Charles, of Kent, eq. Balnei. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1615, aged 16; B.A. 11 May, 1618, student of Inner Temple 1615 (as 4th son of Sir Peter, of St. Stephen's, Canterbury, kt., and grandson of Sir Roger, late lord chief baron, etc.); slain abroad; brother of Roger 1607, and Thomas 1611. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stockton, John, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 20, B.A. 28 Jan., 1618-19, M.A. 11 June, 1621, incorporated at Cambridge 1627; perhaps clerk, M.A., licenced 12 Aug., 1620, to marry Dorothy, daughter of William Barnes, of Lambeth, Surrey, merchant, deceased. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Mason, Thomas, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 20; B.A. 13 Dec., 1615, M.A. 1 July, 1618; perhaps rector of Binbrooke St. Mary, co. Lincoln, 1634, vicar of Great Ashby, co. Leicester; brother of John 1622, and father of Thomas 1661. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Shuckburghe, Richard, of Northants, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1615, aged 19, B.A. 3 May, 1615; of Shuckburgh, co. Warwick, M.P. circa Dec., 1640 (L.P.), till disabled in 1644; knighted 22 Oct., 1642; died 13 June, 1656, aged 60. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lightfoote, John, of London (city), cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 17; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1617, as son and heir of Richard, of Stoke Bruerne, Northants, cler.; brother of the next-named. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Reeve, John, of Bucks, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 13 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 1 July, 1623; one of these names vicar of Barnham 1619, and rector of Shellow Bowells, Essex, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Clarke, Richard, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17; B.A. 10 May, 1619, M.A. 26 June, 1622, B.D. 7 July, 1629, perhaps rector of Sudborough, Northants, 1621, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Arderne, Gabriel, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 June, 1615, aged 18; B.A. 24 April, 1616, vicar of Holy Cross, Westgate, and rector of St. Mildred in Canterbury 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Brett, Owen, of Middlesex, eq. aur. fil. nat. max. Lincoln Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1615, aged 17; student of Lincoln's Inn 1618, as son and heir of Sir John, of Edmonton, Middlesex, knight. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Fletcher, Henry, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 10 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 2 Nov., 1620, vicar of Taynton, Oxon, 1632, and of a portion of Bampton 1648. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Parker, Michael, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 14; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 30 June, 1617, M.A. 5 May, 1620; rector of Desford, co. Leicester, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Silverwood, Martin, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19, B.A. 17 July, 1616; perhaps sequestered to the vicarage of Tilton, co. Leicester, 1647. See Add. MS. 15,671, p. 59.

Sanforde, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 18; perhaps a student of Gray's Inn 1618, as son and heir of John, of city of Bristol, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Slade, Francis, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 5 May, 1615, aged 14, B.A. 4 Nov., 1618, M.A. 11 June, 1621; vicar of Wantage, Berks, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 217.
Noyes, Nathan, of co. Worcester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 17; B.A. 26 Oct., 1616, rector of Coldrington, Wilts, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Whalley, Ralph, of co. Leicester, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; of Norton, co. Leicester (s. William); died 7 Dec., 1638.
Johnson, Richard, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 17 Dec., 1618, M.A. 14 June, 1621.
Danvers, John, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 May, 1615, aged 19; son of Francis, of Swithland, co. Leicester.
Cayley, Matthew, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 15; B.A. 1 July, 1618, M.A. 20 April, 1621.
Godfrey, William, of Berks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 18; B.A. 24 April, 1616, M.A. 28 Jan., 1618-19.
Juckes, Francis, of Salop, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 10 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 10 July, 1623.
Gibbon, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 16; B.A. 1 July, 1618 M.A. 19 April, 1621.
Boylson, Edward, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 31 Jan., 1615-16.
Hill, Robert, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1615-16, aged 19; B.A. 1 Feb., 1615-16.
Soley, Henry, of co. Worcester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1615, aged 20, B.A. 26 Oct., 1615.
Adkins, Thomas, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 16; B.A. 10 Feb., 1617-18.
Churchman, Richard, of Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 16; B.A. 3 July, 1621.
Johnson, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 May, 1615, aged 14; B.A. 31 Jan., 1620-1.
Whitstones, Edward (Whytstones), of Beds, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 5 May, 1615, aged 14.
Poole, Thomas, of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19; B.A. 25 Feb., 1616-17.
Browne, Thomas of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 18. See William 1634.
Southworth, Edmund (Sowthworth), of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 15.
Ashby, Edward (Ashbie), of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 19.
Cooke, Abraham, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17.
Shortgrave, Richard, of Northants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 17.
T(h)omson, Richard, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 16.
Lu(c)kine, William, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 10 Nov., 1615, aged 17.
Dickins, John, of Notts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 5 May, 1615, aged 16.

Gilbert, William, of co. Derby, arm. fil. nat. (2). Lincoln Coll., matric. 31 May, 1616, aged 17; B.A. 5 July, 1620; M.A. from Gloucester Hall 18 June, 1623, "a general scholar and a rare man," of Dublin, surveyor of the plantations (brother of Henry same date); perhaps of Kilminchy, Queen's co. and a knight, governor of the fort of Leix, M.P. Maryborough 1634 and 1639, and for Dublin university 1639, until his death 5 June, 1654, buried 8th, in St. John's, Dublin. See Irish M.P. s & Fasti, i. 411.

Burbie, Edward, of London, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 April, 1616, aged 16; B.A. 25 Oct., 1619, M.A. 26 June, 1622, (incorp. at Cambridge 1625), created D.D. 31 Aug., 1636, vicar of Canewdon, Essex, 1627, rector of East Woodhay, 1629, and of Wonston, Hants, 1631, canon and archdeacon of Winchester 1631. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, i. 493.

Thelwall, Simon, of co. Denbigh, "medici fil." Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 June, 1616, aged 15; B.A. from Hart Hall 29 May, 1619; of Plas-said; a colonel, entered student, of Gray's Inn 1619 (as son of Richard, of Llanbedr, co. Denbigh, gent.); M.P. co. Denbigh 1640 (L.P.) till secluded Dec., 1648, co. Denbigh 1654-5. See Foster's Gray's Inn Register.

Jackeson, John, of Yorks, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 June, 1616, aged 16 (2s. John, rector of Melsonby 1573-1607); master of free school, Richmond, Yorks, 1618-20, rector of Marske, Yorks, 1623-50, sometime of Berwick-upon-Tweed. See Fasti, i. 507; Arch. Æliana, v. 9; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bennet, Thomas (Bennit), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 April, 1616, aged 17; B.A. 25 Oct., 1619, a student of Inner Temple 1620, as son and heir of George, of Queensborough, co. Leicester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Sacheverell, Ferdinando, of co. Derby, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 31 May, 1616, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1618 (as s. Henry, of Hopewell, co. Derby, esq.); died 10 Jan., 1628-9; natural brother of Jacinth. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Wotton, Richard (Wootton), of co. Warwick, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 March, 1616-17, aged 15, B.A. 5 July, 1620; vicar of Kineton 1624, and rector of Warmington, (both) co. Warwick, 1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Masters, Henry, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 March, 1616-7, aged 18; perhaps vicar of Misterton, Somerset, 1633, and of Creech St. Michael, Somerset, 1641 (then M.A.). See Foster's Index Eccl.

Butler, Henry, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1616, aged 16; B.A. 31 Jan., 1620-1, one of these names vicar of Wasperton, co. Warwick, 1628. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rouse, John, of Somerset, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 June, 1616, aged 16, B.A. 10 May, 1620, M.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3, rector of Ashington, Somerset, 1637-63. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gilbert, Henry, of co. Derby, arm. fil. nat. max. Lincoln Coll., matric. 31 May, 1616, aged 18; of Locko, co. Derby (s. Thomas), died 1657; brother of William same date.
Ive, William, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 26 April, 1616, aged 18; one of these names M. P. Leicester 1624-5. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Robertes, John, of co. Leicester, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1616, aged 16; B.A. 5 July, 1620, M.A. 30 June, 1624. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thomas, Hugh, of co. Carmarthen, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1616, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 7 Feb., 1620-1.
Knowler, John, of Kent, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 12 April, 1616, aged 17; B.A. 25 Oct., 1619, M.A. 26 June, 1622.
Jessop, Renewed, of Surrey, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1616, aged 17; sup. for B.A. 19 June, 1620.
Jervis, William, of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1616, aged 16.
Gore, John (? Gove), Southants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1616, aged 16.
Cobbett, Thomas, of co. Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1616, aged 16.

Knightley, Thomas, of Northants, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1617, aged 17; B.A. 31 Jan., 1620-1, M.A. 1 July, 1623 (as William), B.D. 26 May, 1631, licenced to preach 1631 (5s. Thomas, of Burghall, co. Stafford), rector of Byfield, Northants, 1631-88; buried there 17 Oct., 1688; father of Richard 1654. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Benyon, Richard, of Essex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 April, 1617, aged 12; B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1626, as son and heir of Richard, late of Coggeshall, Essex, gent., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Collins, Walter, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 27 June, 1617, aged 16; B.A. 31 Jan., 1620-1; M.A. from Broadgates Hall 1 July, 1623, rector of Ashhurst 1635, and of Newenden, Kent, 1664-8. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Daniell, Alexander, of Cornwall, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1617, aged 17; 'diarist' (son of Richard), of Penzance and Laregon, buried in Madron Churchyard. See D.N.B.; Boase & Courtney's Bibl. Cornub., 103, 1146-7.

Hobson, Hugh, of Yorks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1617, aged 17; B.A. 11 June, 1621, M.A. 30 June, 1624, rector of Bristol St. Stephen 1628, and vicar of Tickenham, Somerset, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Coward, Thomas, of Somerset, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric., 9 May, 1617, aged 17; of the Middle Temple 1619, as son of Thomas, of Wells, Somerset. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ford, David (Foord), of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 14 Nov., 1617, aged 17; B.A. 19 April, 1621, rector of Thaydon, Dorset, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chambre, Arthur, of Salop, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1617, aged 17; of Petton, son of Arthur and father of Henry.
Chandler, William (Chantler), of co. Leicester, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 May, 1617, aged 17; B.A. 23 Feb., 1621-2.
Pallmer, Thomas, of Rutland, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 May, 1617, aged 15; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 12 Dec., 1620.
Corker, Nicholas, of London, 'generosae conditionis.' Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 March, 1617-18, aged 17.
Weston, Robert, of Essex, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 11 April, 1617, aged 15, B.A. 11 June, 1621.
Mansell, Thomas (Maunsell), of Oxon, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 May, 1617, aged 16.
Smith, William, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 19 May, 1617, aged 17.
Lye, John, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 March, 1617-8, aged 17.

Culme, Hugh, of Devon, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 18; of the Inner Temple 1622, (as son and heir of Richard, of Cannon Leigh, Devon), baptized 11 May, 1601, at Molland, and buried at Burlescombe 20 March, 1643-4, father of Richard 1652. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Ottley, (Sir) Francis, of Salop, arm. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1618, aged 17; student of the Inner Temple 1620 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Pitchford, Salop, esq.), perhaps knighted 21 Sept., 1642; died 11 Sept., 1649; brother of Richard. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Warde, John, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 9 May, 1622, M.A. 18 June, 1625; vicar of Spratton, Northants, 1626, buried there 18 April, 1660; father of John 1646. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jenkes, Harbart, of Salop, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 May, 1618, aged 16; B.A. 10 May, 1620, possibly bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1638 (as son and heir of Francis, of New Hall, Salop). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Matking, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 19; B.A. 23 Feb., 1621-2; vicar of Barneby, Notts, 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knowler, Richard, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Jan., 1618-19, aged 16 (subs. "Knoller"), B.A. 26 June, 1622; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 3 May, 1625.
Starre, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry 16 Oct., 1618, aged 14, B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 6 July, 1625.
Sanderson, Robert, of Notts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. entry dated 16 Oct., 1618, aged 13; perhaps father of the next-named.
Walweyn, Harbart, of co. Worcester, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 May, 1618, aged 18, B.A. 14 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 2 June, 1624.
Bourne, Robert, of co. Stafford, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 18; B.A. 14 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 2 June, 1624.
Basely, John, of Northants, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1618, aged 17; B.A. 26 June, 1622, M.A. 3 May, 1625.
Ottley, Richard, of Salop, arm. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1618, aged 16; B.A. 14 Feb., 1621-2, M.A. 2 June, 1624.
Fewkes, Thomas (Fukes), of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 17; B.A. 23 Feb., 1621-2.
Eyre, Richard, of co. York, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1618, aged 18; B.A. 26 June, 1622.
Furse, Zachariah (Fursse), of Cornwall, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1618, aged 19.
Howse, Joseph, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1618, aged 17.
Mekin, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 3 July, 1618, aged 17.
Young, Richard, of Wilts, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1618-19, aged 16.

Smith, Thomas, of Beds, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1619, aged 17, B.A. 7 Nov., 1622, M.A. 27 June, 1625, B. and D.Med. and licenced to practice medicine 24 July, 1633, perhaps created D.Med. 1 Nov., 1642; brother of James 1622-3.

Kilbie, Robert, of co. Leicester, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1619, aged 18; B.A. 26 June, 1622, M.A. 3 May, 1625; student of Gray's Inn 1620, as of Ratcliffe-super-Wreak, co. Leicester. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Uppington, John, of Somerset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 15, B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 23 June, 1625; rector of Withycombe, Somerset, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lovell, George, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 16; B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3; M.A. from Hart Hall 21 June, 1625, rector of Chettle, Dorset, 1638. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Garthwait, Thomas, of Lincoln, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 28 Jan., 1619-20, aged 16; baptised at Fulbeck 17 April, 1603; brother of Ephraim.
Haynes, Edward, of Salop, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 16; B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3; M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 23 June, 1625.
Busby, John (Busbie), of Bucks, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1619, aged 16; of Addington, Bucks, brother of Robert 1618.
Westfalinge, Harbert, of co. Hereford, arm. fil. nat. max. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1619-20, aged 17, B.A. 26 June, 1622.
Macham, John, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1619, aged 18; B.A. 1 March, 1621-2.
Handleighe, John, of Dorset, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 18.
Swetnam, Laurence, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 16.
Sawrey, Antony, of Bucks, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1619-20, aged 15.
Loope, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 23 April, 1619, aged 18.
Baker, John, of Sussex, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1619, aged 16.
Kipping, Robert, of Kent, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1620, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1623, as son and heir of Walter, of Tudeley, Kent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Fleetwood, George, of Beds, militis fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 16; B.A. 6 Feb., 1623-4, student of Gray's Inn 1620, (as 2s. of Miles, a knight and receiver of the court of wards), baptised at Cople, Beds, 30 June, 1605, Swedish general, created a baron by Queen Christina 1 June, 1654; died in that country 11 June, 1667; brother of Roger 1628. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.; D.N.B.; & Lipscomb's Bucks, iii. 228.

Hastings, Charles, of co. Worcester. cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 15; B.A. 2 June, 1624, M.A. 17 April, 1627; rector of Yelford, Oxon, 1625; probably son of Simon Hastings by his second wife. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Visitation of Oxford; & Worcester Visitation 1682-3, p. 54.

Calverly, Henry, of co. York, esq. (2nd son). Lincoln Coll., matric. 16 Nov., 1621, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1623 (as son and heir of Walter, of Calverley, Yorks, deceased), compounded for his loyalty, born 21 Sept., 1604, buried at Calverley 2 Jan., 1651. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Kinaston, Samuel, of Salop, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 16, B.A. 3 Feb., 1624-5; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 11 July, 1628, incorporated at Cambridge 1634, rector of Blackmanston. Kent, 1630, of Somerford Magna, Wilts, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Sacheverell, Clarence, of co. Leicester, arm. fil. (3). Lincoln Coll., matric. 8 June, 1621, aged 21 (subscribes "Clare,") B.A. 11 June, 1621; (3s. Francis, of Reresby), rector of Rearsby, Leicester 1636, as Clare. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Watson, James, of Devon, cler. fil. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18, B.A. 19 Feb., 1622-3, M.A. 27 June, 1625; vicar of Aylisbeare 1629, and of Ermington, (both) Devon, 1639, and father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Primatt, Stephen, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18, B.A. 25 Nov., 1624; incorporated at Cambridge 1628; M.A. from Peter House 1629, rector of Denton, co. Lincoln, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hornsey, Thomas, of co. Lincoln, gent. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 18; demy Magdalen Coll. 1621-31, B.A. 6 Dec., 1623, M.A. 27 June, 1626, rector of Broughton Pogis, Oxon, 1638. See Bloxam, v. 107.

Ashton, William, of Middlesex, arm. fil. nat. max. Lincoln Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1621, aged 16; B.A. 6 Feb., 1623-4 (? created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642, as Aston). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Alumni West., 90.
